tagline: Calculate gravel volume, weight, and cost by type
title: Gravel Calculator — How Much Gravel Do I Need? (Tons & Yards)
metaDescription: Calculate tons and cubic yards of gravel for driveways, paths, and landscaping. Supports pea gravel, crushed stone, and river rock with cost estimates.
keywords: gravel calculator, how much gravel do I need, gravel tonnage calculator, crushed stone calculator, pea gravel calculator, gravel driveway calculator

howItWorks.intro: Gravel is sold by the ton or cubic yard. Knowing the volume and weight helps you order the right amount and estimate delivery costs.
rules: Volume: Area (sq ft) × Depth (inches) / 12 = cubic feet. Divide by 27 for cubic yards
Weight varies by type: crushed stone ~2,800 lbs/yd³, pea gravel ~2,600 lbs/yd³, river rock ~3,000 lbs/yd³
For driveways, use 4-6 inches of gravel. Compact in 2-inch lifts for best results
howItWorks.outro: Always use landscape fabric under decorative gravel to prevent weed growth. For driveways, layer different sizes: larger crushed stone as a base (4"), then a finer top layer (2"). Compact each layer before adding the next.

faq.question: How much does a ton of gravel cover?
faq.answer: One ton of gravel covers approximately 80 square feet at 4 inches deep, 108 sq ft at 3 inches, or 160 sq ft at 2 inches. Coverage varies slightly by gravel type and size — smaller stones pack more tightly and cover slightly less area.

faq.question: How much gravel do I need for a driveway?
faq.answer: A 10' x 40' driveway at 4 inches deep requires about 4.9 cubic yards or roughly 7 tons of gravel. For a proper gravel driveway, plan for 4-6 inches total depth in multiple layers: 3-4 inches of #57 stone base topped with 2 inches of finer gravel.

faq.question: What type of gravel is best for driveways?
faq.answer: Crushed stone (#57 or #411) is best for driveways — angular pieces interlock and compact well. Top with a finer crusher run (#21A) for a smooth surface. Avoid round pea gravel for driveways — it shifts under tires and does not compact. River rock is decorative only.

faq.question: How much does a cubic yard of gravel weigh?
faq.answer: Crushed stone: ~2,800 lbs (1.4 tons). Pea gravel: ~2,600 lbs (1.3 tons). River rock: ~3,000 lbs (1.5 tons). Lava rock: ~2,500 lbs (1.25 tons). Wet gravel can weigh 10-15% more. A dump truck typically carries 12-15 tons.

faq.question: Should I put landscape fabric under gravel?
faq.answer: Yes, always use heavy-duty landscape fabric under decorative gravel and pathways. It prevents weed growth and stops gravel from sinking into the soil. Use commercial-grade fabric (not cheap weed barrier) and overlap seams by 6 inches. For driveways, geotextile fabric is even better.

faq.question: How deep should gravel be for a patio or walkway?
faq.answer: Walkways: 2-3 inches of compacted gravel over landscape fabric. Patios: 3-4 inches. Use a finer gravel (#89 or pea gravel) for the surface and a coarser base layer for drainage. Edge with steel, aluminum, or stone edging to keep gravel contained.
